prompt: Somber, textured oil painting rendered in a documentary photography style, viewed from a high-angle, macro-scale perspective looking down at a fragmented, excavation landscape that was once a child’s bed. This is an affective data visualization. The central focus is a massive, monolithic black polished obsidian slab (the phone’s screen), standing as a colossal tombstone crushing the heart of the sleeping area. It is impossibly large, consuming over three-quarters of the bed's structure. On the left side, representing the 'halved sleep' feeling, a tiny, fractured sliver of a soft white pillow desperately exists, visibly being eclipsed and crushed by the edge of the obsidian monolith. A few desaturated, almost invisible whispers of dream-like light tendrils fade around it. On the right side, representing the 'doubled burden,' an overwhelmingly vast, complex, multi-layered 'fortress-density' of rusted, intertwined iron chains coils and twists, forming a physical architecture of inescapable confinement and moral weight, occupying all remaining space. The scale difference between the massive block and the dense fortress of chains is overwhelming. The cold, sterile cyan light from the edge of the obsidian slab is the only sharp illumination, cutting into the deep shadows. Color palette: suffocating midnight blues, deep charcoal grey, ochre-brown, and a single piercing, cold cyan digital glow. Mood: inescapable confinement, profound imbalance, morally heavy finality. No text, no labels, no numbers, no people, single frame composition.
dataset_name: CDC Youth Risk Behavior Survey (Screen time vs sleep in children)
insight: Excessive screen time consumes over three-quarters of a child's nightly environment, effectively halving their restful sleep while doubling their psychological burden.
